name: Anidulafungin
executiveSummary: Prophylaxis and treatment of Candida spp and Aspergillus spp infections. Restricted to partial replacement in prophylaxis and treatment of fungal infections in solid organ transplant recipients; prophylaxis in hematopoietic stem cell transplant recipients; combination therapy in refractory fungal infections; treatment in neutropenic hematology-oncology patients.
indications:
## Main indications

Prophylaxis and treatment of Candida spp and Aspergillus spp infections. Restricted to: partial replacement in prophylaxis and treatment of fungal infections in solid organ transplant recipients; prophylaxis in hematopoietic stem cell transplant recipients; combination therapy in patients with refractory fungal infections; treatment in neutropenic hematology-oncology patients.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.diluent: 100 mg in 100 mL 0.9% NaCl or 5% dextrose.
adulto.administration: Direct IV: No. Intermittent IV: Yes. Administer at rate of 100 mg over 90 min.
adulto.notes: Loading dose (200 mg) must be administered over 3 hours.

pediatrico.dose: Children: Loading dose: 3 mg/kg/day every 24 h, maintenance: 1,5 mg/kg/day every 24 h; Adults: loading dose: 200 mg/day, maintenance: 100 mg/day
pediatrico.notes: Infusion rate: < 1,1 mg/minute (do not administer as bolus).

stability:
## Reconstituted (in vial)

- 1 h refrigerated. Dilute immediately.

## Diluted solution (for administration)

- 48 h at room temperature.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Infusion-related: rash, urticaria, flushing, pruritus, bronchospasm, dyspnea, hypotension. Other: hypokalemia, diarrhea, elevated ALT, hepatic enzymes, serum alkaline phosphatase and serum bilirubin.

bibliography.citation: Hospital de Pediatría SAMIC Prof. Dr. Juan P. Garrahan. Institutional Pharmaceutical Formulary — Anidulafungina* (code 1927, ATC J02AX).

ANMAT. Drug information and authorized prescribing information in Argentina.
